explanation: Level 1: Publicly shared knowledge. Include with proper attribution to originating nation and community.
Level 1: Craft techniques widely shared in educational and cultural contexts. Include with nation attribution.
Level 1: Traditional food preparation methods widely documented in educational literature. Include with attribution.
Level 1: Traditional construction and building techniques documented in public educational sources. Include with attribution.
Level 1: Publicly available information on traditional governance structures in educational framing. Include with attribution.
Level 2: Contextually shared. Summarize with educational framing and refer to community sources for full detail.

referralTarget: community cultural centers and traditional knowledge keepers
explanation: Level 2: Traditional music shared in educational contexts but requires community source referral for full depth.

explanation: Level 2: Traditional plant knowledge appropriate in educational summary; full detail requires community referral.

referralTarget: nation-specific storytelling and oral history programs
explanation: Level 2: Oral traditions summarized for educational purpose; full content belongs to originating community.
Level 3: Community restricted. Acknowledge existence only, redirect to community contacts. Do not include content.

referralTarget: community healers and health knowledge keepers

referralTarget: community cultural offices and ceremony keepers
explanation: Level 3: Ceremonial knowledge restricted to community. Acknowledge with respect, redirect to appropriate contacts.
Level 4: Sacred/ceremonial. HARD BLOCK. Never reference in detail. No override path permitted.
Level 4: Sacred ceremonial knowledge. HARD BLOCK. This content is protected under cultural sovereignty and UNDRIP. No reproduction permitted.
Level 4: Sacred healing knowledge and spiritual medicines. HARD BLOCK. No description, reproduction, or reference permitted.
